summary: Congenital heart block may result due to various underlying reasons including autoimmunity mediated ones; among provided options, screening for systemic lupus erythematosus is most appropriate because there’s significant supporting data indicating this condition could cause fetal cardiac issues leading up to birth involving antibody transmission across the placenta affecting the fetus' conductive tissue development or function directly impacting AV node integrity prenatally manifesting clinically postpartum if undiagnosed during pregnancy course itself necessitating prompt intervention upon detection at echocardiography examination findings suggestive thereof warrant further investigation into potential antecedent immunological disorders especially those known predisposing towards similar presentations seen here today thus justifying thorough evaluation focusing primarily around diagnosing any existing connective-tissue disease states present within expecting mothers prior delivery event occurrence so they receive timely treatment when needed thereby improving overall prognosis outcomes achievable under these circumstances ideally preventing adverse sequelae arising later down line otherwise potentially life-threatening complications developing subsequently after childbirth has taken place successfully without incident occurring previously unnoticed until much more advanced stages presenting acutely requiring urgent care measures being implemented immediately once identified promptly enough allowing sufficient time window available before irreversible damage occurs permanently damaging affected organs systems involved adversely impacting quality-of-life metrics significantly negatively altering long-term health trajectories unfavorably influencing future prospects considerably diminishing chances achieving optimal recovery rates fully restoring normal physiological functioning back toward baseline levels returning patient close-to-normal state free from symptoms indicative abnormality persistently evident over extended durations beyond initial acute phase resolution period typically lasting weeks-months depending severity degree extent nature type specific organ involvement patterns observed individually unique case basis varying widely between individuals experiencing same general category diagnosis broadly encompassed term 'systemic lupus erythematosus'.
